EGD + Colonoscopy Prep

{{BAND_LABEL}}{{BAND_LB}}
{{BAND_NOTE}}
Procedure Location
{{LOCATION_NAME}}
{{LOCATION_ADDRESS}}
{{LOCATION_PHONE_LABEL}}: {{LOCATION_PHONE}}
{{LOCATION_ARRIVAL}}

🔎 What Are These Procedures?

Your child is scheduled for two procedures done back-to-back while asleep under anesthesia:

  • EGD (upper endoscopy) — a thin camera is passed through the mouth to look at the esophagus, stomach, and the first part of the small intestine. Small biopsies are usually taken.
  • Colonoscopy — the same kind of camera is passed through the bottom to look at the large intestine. Biopsies are usually taken here too.

Both procedures together take about 30–60 minutes. Your child will not feel anything.

📖 About the Bowel Prep

The colon must be clean for the doctor to see clearly during the colonoscopy. The medicine below empties it before the procedure. The EGD itself does not need bowel prep — only the fasting times.

✅ Plan Ahead (buy at least 2 days before)

Pick up these items at least 2 days before the procedure.

Active ingredient / ItemWhat to get
SUPREP Bowel Prep Kit (sodium sulfate, potassium sulfate, magnesium sulfate) — prescription required (FDA-approved age 12+)
Call the office if you have not received a prescription. The kit includes a mixing container. SUPREP must be diluted in water before drinking — do NOT drink it straight from the bottle.		 1 kit
Contains {{HTML_SUPREP_TOTAL_BOTTLES}} bottles ({{HTML_SUPREP_BOTTLE_OZ}} oz each) — each bottle = 1 dose
Clear liquids — water, Gatorade or Powerade (any color except red or purple), apple juice, white grape juice, clear broth At least 72 oz (~2 L) total (in addition to the water used to dilute SUPREP). Include a balanced electrolyte drink (Gatorade, Pedialyte, or similar).
Skin protection — protective ointment (Desitin or Vaseline) for anal-area comfort 1 tube
⚠️ Do NOT give Dulcolax (bisacodyl) or any other laxative with this prep. SUPREP already contains sulfate-based stimulant salts; combining stimulant laxatives can increase the risk of mucosal injury or ischemic colitis.
⚠️ 💊 Medications

Prepare a list of medications and be ready to discuss them with the nurse or scheduler when they call.
Stop {{HTML_MEDICATIONS_DRUGS}} 7 days before.
{{HTML_SUPREP_DRUG_INTERACTION_NOTE}}
Do not give other oral medications within {{HTML_SUPREP_ORAL_MEDS_BLACKOUT_HOURS}} hour of starting each SUPREP dose.
You can verify what medications may need to be stopped at meds.giready.com.

{{HTML_PRECLEANOUT_BLOCK}}

📅 3 Days Before — Low-Residue Diet

AVOID OK to eat
Whole grains, nuts, seeds White rice, pasta, potatoes (no skin)
Raw fruits and vegetables Bananas, melons, cooked veggies
Corn, beans, popcorn Eggs, chicken, fish, yogurt, pudding

📅 1 Day Before — Clear Liquids All Day

Clear liquids only — no solid food, no dairy for the entire day.

AVOID OK to drink
ALL solid food and dairy Water, Gatorade/Powerade (not red/purple)
RED or PURPLE drinks or foods Apple juice, white grape juice
Alcohol; juices with pulp Lemonade, clear soda, clear broth; popsicles, plain Jell-O (not red/purple); tea/coffee without milk

🥤 DOSE 1 — Evening Before the Procedure

SUPREP tastes salty/tart. Drinking it cold and through a straw can help.

Between {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE1_WINDOW}}
Pour one bottle of SUPREP ({{HTML_SUPREP_BOTTLE_OZ}} oz) into the mixing container that comes with the kit. Add cool drinking water to the {{HTML_SUPREP_FILL_LINE_OZ}}-oz fill line. Mix, then drink the entire {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE1_SOLUTION_OZ}} oz.
After the dose — next {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE1_CHASERS_HOURS}} hour
Drink {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE1_CHASER_FILLS}} more containers of plain water, each filled to the {{HTML_SUPREP_FILL_LINE_OZ}}-oz line — {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE1_CHASERS_OZ}} oz total. Drink steadily; don't sip slowly over the whole hour.
💡 Tips for Dose 1
  • SUPREP tastes salty/tart. Cold water + a straw helps; some patients chase each sip with clear Gatorade or lemonade to cut the taste.
  • Drink each portion steadily — don't sip slowly over a long time.
  • Expect diarrhea to start within 1–3 hours — this is normal and means the prep is working.
  • Apply protective ointment to the bottom area before diarrhea starts.
  • Continue drinking clear liquids throughout the evening to stay hydrated.
  • If severe bloating, distension, or abdominal pain occurs after Dose 1, slow down and contact the office before starting Dose 2.

🥤 DOSE 2 — Morning of the Procedure

{{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE_SEPARATION_MIN}}–{{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE_SEPARATION_MAX}} hours after Dose 1 (and at least {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE2_HOURS_BEFORE_MIN}} hours before procedure)
Pour the second bottle of SUPREP ({{HTML_SUPREP_BOTTLE_OZ}} oz) into the mixing container, add cool water to the {{HTML_SUPREP_FILL_LINE_OZ}}-oz fill line, mix, and drink the entire {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE2_SOLUTION_OZ}} oz.
After the dose — next {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE2_CHASERS_HOURS}} hour
Drink {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE2_CHASER_FILLS}} more containers of plain water, each filled to the {{HTML_SUPREP_FILL_LINE_OZ}}-oz line — {{HTML_SUPREP_DOSE2_CHASERS_OZ}} oz total. Complete all SUPREP solution and required water at least {{NPO_CLEARS_HOURS}} hours before the procedure.

⚠️ Stop all clear liquids {{NPO_CLEARS_HOURS}} hours before the procedure. If your child vomits within 30 minutes of a dose, wait 30 minutes and try giving the dose again slowly.

📅 Day of Procedure

  • Clear liquids only. No solid food.
  • Stop all clear liquids {{NPO_CLEARS_HOURS}} hours before the procedure.
  • The EGD is done first (through the mouth), then the colonoscopy. Both happen during the same nap.
  • Bring comfort items for your child (toy, blanket, tablet, etc.).
⚠️ If the bowel prep is not followed exactly, the colonoscopy may be delayed or canceled. The EGD does not need bowel prep, but the fasting rules apply to both.

Helpful Resources

▶ Watch a video about the procedure Example only — follow this handout, not the video. 📨 Patient portal Message us — preferred contact method 🌐 GIKids.org General info on pediatric GI procedures

🍚 Low-Residue Diet — Sample Meals

Meal ideas for the low-residue (“white”) diet. Continue this diet for the 3 days before the procedure. The day before the procedure, switch to clear liquids only — no dairy.

Breakfast (morning)

  • Scrambled eggs
  • White toast with butter
  • Banana
  • Yogurt (no seeds or berries)
  • Apple juice

Lunch (~noon)

  • Plain chicken sandwich on white bread (no veggies)
  • Mac and cheese
  • White rice with chicken
  • Plain pasta
  • Applesauce
  • Vanilla pudding

After lunch (~2:00 PM onward)

  • Clear liquids only (see allowed drinks above)
  • No more dairy — milk, yogurt, ice cream, etc.
After the procedure & when to call
  • Your child will wake up in the recovery area. Some grogginess and gas/cramping/bloating are normal for several hours.
  • A small amount of blood with the first stool after a colonoscopy can be normal, especially if biopsies were taken.
  • An adult must stay with your child for 24 hours after anesthesia.
  • Start with clear liquids, then advance to a regular diet as tolerated.
  • No school, daycare, or sports for the rest of the day. Most children are back to normal activity the next day.
  • The doctor will discuss what was seen before you go home. Biopsy results take about 1–2 weeks; we'll send the results to the portal and discuss over the phone or at the next appointment.

Call (317) 338-9450 if your child has any of the following after the procedure:

  • A temperature over 101°F (38.3°C)
  • Stool that is black or has more than a small streak of blood
  • Severe or worsening abdominal pain, or a hard, swollen belly
  • Persistent vomiting, or refusal to drink
  • Difficulty breathing or chest pain — this is an emergency, call 911
For patients of Dr. Deivanayagam, Dr. Dunn, Dr. Schaefer, Dr. Tibesar, and Dr. Zavoian — Pediatric Gastroenterology at Peyton Manning Children's Hospital in Indianapolis. Not a substitute for medical advice.
Office: (317) 338-9450
